# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project

`tallyo` is a Next.js (App Router) UI for viewing sports games with live updates, game details, standings, and league dashboards. Supported leagues: `nfl`, `cfb`, `nhl`, `mls`. Deployed on Vercel at https://tallyo.us.

The backend is a Spring Boot service in the sibling repo `../tallyo-backend`, which is the source of truth for the API contract (`../tallyo-backend/openapi.yaml`). This repo has no database and no backend logic of its own — it's a thin proxy + presentation layer.

## Commands

- Dev server: `npm run dev` (uses `--turbopack`)
- Build: `npm run build`
- Lint/fix: `npm run lint` (eslint --fix)
- Typecheck: `npx tsc --noEmit`
- Regenerate API types from backend OpenAPI spec: `npm run gen:api-types` (reads `../tallyo-backend/openapi.yaml`, requires the sibling repo checked out; writes `src/types/api.generated.ts`)
- Verify generated types match the OpenAPI contract (used in CI): `npm run check:api-contract`

There is no test suite configured in this repo — validation is lint + typecheck (+ build).

Backend (reference only, in `../tallyo-backend`): `./mvnw -q -DskipTests compile` to compile, `./mvnw test` to test.

## Architecture

### Data flow
UI components call Next.js API routes under `src/app/api/*` (`/api/games`, `/api/games/current`, `/api/context`, `/api/standings`, `/api/stats`, `/api/leagues`) via SWR. These routes are thin proxies: they read query params, forward the request to `${BACKEND_URL}/api/v1/...` with an `x-api-key` header (`API_KEY` env var), and pass the backend's status/body straight through on error (don't swallow or reshape backend errors — see `src/app/api/games/route.ts` for the pattern). Some routes (odds, standings, stats) instead go through `src/lib/espn/espnService.ts`, which calls ESPN endpoints directly and maps the response into internal types via `src/lib/espn/mappers/*`.

### Key source-of-truth files
- `src/lib/leagues/leagueConfig.ts` — per-league capability flags (`supportsStandings`, `supportsOdds`, `supportsLiveDetails`, `teamOrder`, season/week/year UI options, stats profile). Backend-provided `LeagueMetadata` is merged with local `UI_PRESETS`/`DEFAULT_LEAGUE_METADATA` fallbacks. Note: `supportsStandings` is force-enabled for `nhl`/`mls` in `buildLeagueConfigs` regardless of backend metadata.
- `src/lib/gameStatus.ts` — single source of truth for scheduled/live/final status logic (`isScheduledGame`, `isInProgressGame`, `isFinalGame`, `isLiveDashboardGame`, etc.). Always use these helpers rather than comparing `gameStatus` strings inline.
- `src/lib/api/fetcher.ts` — shared SWR fetcher; throws on non-2xx and attaches `status`/`code`/`details` from the API error envelope.
- `src/app/[league]/page.tsx` — dynamic league route; works for any league id present in league config, no per-league routing needed.
- `src/components/SportPage.tsx` — main per-league screen implementation.
- `src/proxy.ts` — Next.js middleware; redirects all routes to `/maintenance` when `NEXT_PUBLIC_MAINTENANCE_MODE=true`.

### API contract
- Contract source: `../tallyo-backend/openapi.yaml`.
- Generated types: `src/types/api.generated.ts` (do not hand-edit; regenerate via `npm run gen:api-types`).
- App-facing aliases/shortcuts over the generated types: `src/types/api-contract.ts`.
- Expected API error shape: `{ code: string; message: string; details?: string; path?: string; timestamp?: string }`.
- CI (`.github/workflows/contract-check.yml`) checks generated types are in sync with the OpenAPI spec via `npm run check:api-contract`.

### Timezone handling
The UI resolves the browser's local timezone and passes it to Next API routes as `timezone`; Next routes forward it to the backend as `userTimeZone`. Any new endpoint whose response depends on date boundaries (today's games, current context, etc.) must accept and forward timezone.

### Adding a new sport/league
1. Add the league to `src/lib/leagues/leagueConfig.ts` (`DEFAULT_LEAGUE_METADATA`, and a `UI_PRESETS` entry if it needs season/week/year filtering).
2. No new route needed — the dynamic `[league]` route picks it up once config exists.
3. Add/wire a stats display map in `src/lib/espn/enums/statDisplayMaps.ts` if the league needs a new stats profile.
4. Add the league on the backend (enum + data fetch support) and confirm `/games`, `/games/current`, `/context`, `/dates` behave as expected.
5. Update `../tallyo-backend/openapi.yaml` if request/response shape changed, then run `npm run gen:api-types`.
6. Validate: `npm run lint && npx tsc --noEmit`.

## Conventions

- Prefer central config (`leagueConfig.ts`) over hardcoded per-league `if (league === "nfl")` branching.
- Prefer the shared helpers in `gameStatus.ts` over re-deriving status/date logic inline.
- Keep proxy API routes transparent on errors — forward the backend's status and body rather than wrapping them.
- Keep `src/types/api-contract.ts` aligned with the OpenAPI-generated types; regenerate rather than hand-editing generated types.

result.output:
1	#!/usr/bin/env ruby
2	# frozen_string_literal: true
3	
4	require "yaml"
5	
6	input_path = ARGV[0]
7	output_path = ARGV[1]
8	
9	if input_path.nil? || output_path.nil?
10	  warn "Usage: ruby scripts/generate-api-types.rb <openapi.yaml> <output.ts>"
11	  exit 1
12	end
13	
14	unless File.exist?(input_path)
15	  warn "OpenAPI file not found: #{input_path}"
16	  exit 1
17	end
18	
19	doc = YAML.load_file(input_path)
20	schemas = doc.dig("components", "schemas") || {}
21	
22	primitive_map = {
23	  "string" => "string",
24	  "integer" => "number",
25	  "number" => "number",
26	  "boolean" => "boolean",
27	}
28	
29	def indent(level)
30	  "  " * level
31	end
32	
33	def ts_type_for(schema, primitive_map, required: false, level: 0)
34	  return "unknown" if schema.nil? || !schema.is_a?(Hash)
35	
36	  if schema["$ref"]
37	    ref_name = schema["$ref"].split("/").last
38	    return "components[\"schemas\"][\"#{ref_name}\"]"
39	  end
40	
41	  if schema["enum"].is_a?(Array) && !schema["enum"].empty?
42	    return schema["enum"].map { |value| value.is_a?(String) ? "\"#{value}\"" : value.inspect }.join(" | ")
43	  end
44	
45	  if schema["oneOf"].is_a?(Array)
46	    return schema["oneOf"].map { |item| ts_type_for(item, primitive_map, level: level) }.join(" | ")
47	  end
48	
49	  if schema["anyOf"].is_a?(Array)
50	    return schema["anyOf"].map { |item| ts_type_for(item, primitive_map, level: level) }.join(" | ")
51	  end
52	
53	  type = schema["type"]
54	  case type
55	  when "array"
56	    items = schema["items"].is_a?(Hash) ? schema["items"] : {}
57	    return "(#{ts_type_for(items, primitive_map, level: level)})[]"
58	  when "object"
59	    if schema["additionalProperties"].is_a?(Hash)
60	      value_type = ts_type_for(schema["additionalProperties"], primitive_map, level: level)
61	      return "{ [key: string]: #{value_type} }"
62	    end
63	
64	    properties = schema["properties"]
65	    return "Record<string, unknown>" unless properties.is_a?(Hash)
66	
67	    required_props = (schema["required"] || []).to_set
68	    lines = ["{"]
69	    properties.each do |name, prop_schema|
70	      optional = required_props.include?(name) ? "" : "?"
71	      prop_type = ts_type_for(prop_schema, primitive_map, required: required_props.include?(name), level: level + 1)
72	      lines << "#{indent(level + 1)}\"#{name}\"#{optional}: #{prop_type};"
73	    end
74	    lines << "#{indent(level)}}"
75	    return lines.join("\n")
76	  else
77	    return primitive_map.fetch(type, "unknown")
78	  end
79	end
80	
81	require "set"
82	
83	output_lines = []
84	output_lines << "// AUTO-GENERATED FILE. DO NOT EDIT."
85	output_lines << "// Generated from OpenAPI schema. Run `npm run gen:api-types`."
86	output_lines << ""
87	output_lines << "export interface components {"
88	output_lines << "  schemas: {"
89	
90	schemas.each do |name, schema|
91	  schema_type = ts_type_for(schema, primitive_map, level: 2)
92	  schema_declaration =
93	    if schema_type.include?("\n")
94	      schema_type.lines.map.with_index do |line, idx|
95	        idx.zero? ? "    \"#{name}\": #{line}" : "    #{line}"
96	      end.join
97	    else
98	      "\"#{name}\": #{schema_type};"
99	    end
100	
101	  if schema_declaration.end_with?("}")
102	    schema_declaration += ";"
103	  end
104	
105	  output_lines << "    #{schema_declaration}".gsub("    \"#{name}\":     ", "    \"#{name}\": ")
106	end
107	
108	output_lines << "  };"
109	output_lines << "}"
110	output_lines << ""
111	
112	File.write(output_path, output_lines.join("\n"))
113	puts "Generated #{output_path} from #{input_path}"
114

name: Contract Check

on:
  push:
  pull_request:
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  frontend-contract:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Frontend
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Checkout Backend Spec
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          repository: jpotter185/tallyo-backend
          path: tallyo-backend

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Setup Ruby
        uses: ruby/setup-ruby@v1
        with:
          ruby-version: "3.2"

      - name: Verify generated API types are up to date
        env:
          OPENAPI_PATH: tallyo-backend/openapi.yaml
        run: npm run check:api-contract

      - name: Lint and typecheck
        run: |
          npm run lint
          npx tsc --noEmit

input.content:
---
name: verify
description: Launch the Tallyo Next.js frontend against a local backend and drive league flows end-to-end.
---

# Verify Tallyo frontend changes

## Backend dependency

Start the sibling backend first — see
`../tallyo-backend/.claude/skills/verify/SKILL.md` (throwaway Postgres +
`./mvnw spring-boot:run`, ready in ~15s, seeds real ESPN data on boot).

## Launch

`.env.local` points at prod; override with real env vars (they take
precedence over .env files in Next.js):

```bash
BACKEND_URL=http://localhost:8080 API_KEY=<backend key> npm run dev -- --port 3300
```

## Drive

The Next API routes are unauthenticated proxies:

```bash
curl -s localhost:3300/api/leagues                     # league ids from backend metadata
curl -s "localhost:3300/api/games/current?league=<id>&timezone=America/New_York"
curl -s "localhost:3300/api/standings?league=<id>"
curl -s "localhost:3300/api/games/<gameId>/details"
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" localhost:3300/<league-id>   # SSR page, 200 vs 404
```

## Gotchas

- `/api/games/current` (proxy) returns a bare array, unlike the backend's
  paginated `{content: [...]}` envelope.
- League ids must pass `parseLeagueId` in `src/lib/leagues/leagueConfig.ts`
  (LEAGUE_ID_REGEX) or proxy routes 400 before reaching the backend.
- `src/types/api.generated.ts`: the committed file is prettier-formatted;
  the raw `npm run gen:api-types` output is not. Run
  `npx eslint --fix src/types/api.generated.ts` after regenerating or the
  diff is 300 lines of formatting churn (and `check:api-contract`'s
  raw-diff assumption is broken because of this).
- Repo CLAUDE.md mentions `src/lib/espn/espnService.ts` — that layer no
  longer exists; standings/stats go through the backend proxy now.

content.text:
Frontend is done — the World Cup now flows through the whole stack. The changes in `~/projects/tallyo` are deliberately small because the UI is metadata-driven off the backend's `/api/v1/leagues`:

- **`src/lib/leagues/leagueConfig.ts`** — two changes: added the `world_cup` fallback entry to `DEFAULT_LEAGUE_METADATA` (same shape as MLS: soccer profile, date context, home-left, standings on), and fixed `LEAGUE_ID_REGEX` from `[a-z0-9-]` to `[a-z0-9_-]`. That regex fix was load-bearing — `parseLeagueId` gates both the `[league]` page route and every proxy API route, so without it `world_cup` would have 404'd/400'd everywhere even with correct backend data.
- **`src/types/api.generated.ts`** — regenerated from the updated `openapi.yaml`; the real diff is one line (the league enum now includes `"world_cup"`).
- **`CLAUDE.md`** — supported-leagues list updated.

No new routes or components were needed: the dynamic `[league]` route, header nav, dashboard, standings, and soccer stat display maps all pick the league up from config.

## Verification: `world_cup` end-to-end through the Next.js frontend

**Verdict:** PASS

**Method:** Restarted the scratch Postgres + local backend (data survived: 104 games), ran `npm run dev` on port 3300 with `BACKEND_URL`/`API_KEY` overriding the prod-pointing `.env.local`, and drove the proxy routes and SSR page. Recipe saved to the repo's `.claude/skills/verify/SKILL.md`.

### Steps
1. ✅ `GET /api/leagues` via the Next proxy → `['nfl', 'cfb', 'nhl', 'mls', 'world_cup']`.
2. ✅ `GET /api/games/current?league=world_cup` → today's Round of 16 fixtures (Norway @ Brazil, England @ Mexico, scheduled) — proving the regex fix, since this proxy route rejects the league id before ever reaching the backend.
3. ✅ `GET /api/standings?league=world_cup` → 12 groups, Group A: Mexico, Czechia, South Korea, South Africa.
4. ✅ `GET /world_cup` page → HTTP 200 with "World Cup" rendered in the HTML (previously would have hit `notFound()`).
5. ✅ `GET /api/games/760502/details` → 8 leaders, 3 scoring plays for yesterday's Morocco–Canada match.
6. 🔍 `league=foo_bar` (passes the widened regex, unknown to backend) → clean 400 `Invalid league` passed through the proxy.
7. ✅ `npx tsc --noEmit` and eslint clean.

### Findings
- ⚠️ **`npm run check:api-contract` is broken on master already, before my change.** The committed `api.generated.ts` is prettier-formatted, but the check diffs against the generator's raw (unformatted) output — I confirmed regenerating from the *unchanged* spec also produces a 156-line mismatch. I followed the de-facto pipeline (generate, then `eslint --fix` on the file), which yields the correct one-line diff. Worth fixing the check or the generator separately.
- The frontend `CLAUDE.md` describes an `src/lib/espn/espnService.ts` layer that no longer exists (standings/stats now go through the backend proxy). I left it, but it's stale.
- Backend page-size note: `/api/games?league=world_cup` defaults to 100 per page and there are 104 games — fine for the date-filtered views the UI actually uses.

Both repos have uncommitted changes (backend on `fix/prune-stale-scheduled-games`, frontend on `master` — I'd branch before committing there). Remaining rollout steps when you're ready: commit/PR both repos (backend first, since the frontend's contract CI checks out the backend's `main`), and after the backend deploys, run the one-time stats backfill `POST /api/v1/games?league=world_cup&shouldFetchStats=true` so the ~50 already-played matches get their details.
Update claude.md. Fix the api generation
Two tasks: fix the stale claims in the frontend CLAUDE.md, and make `gen:api-types` produce the committed (prettier) format so `check:api-contract` actually works. Let me verify what's stale first.

input.old_string: UI components call Next.js API routes under `src/app/api/*` (`/api/games`, `/api/games/current`, `/api/context`, `/api/standings`, `/api/stats`, `/api/leagues`) via SWR. These routes are thin proxies: they read query params, forward the request to `${BACKEND_URL}/api/v1/...` with an `x-api-key` header (`API_KEY` env var), and pass the backend's status/body straight through on error (don't swallow or reshape backend errors — see `src/app/api/games/route.ts` for the pattern). Some routes (odds, standings, stats) instead go through `src/lib/espn/espnService.ts`, which calls ESPN endpoints directly and maps the response into internal types via `src/lib/espn/mappers/*`.
input.new_string: UI components call Next.js API routes under `src/app/api/*` (`/api/games`, `/api/games/current`, `/api/games/[gameId]/details`, `/api/context`, `/api/standings`, `/api/leagues`) via SWR. These routes are thin proxies: they read query params, forward the request to `${BACKEND_URL}/api/v1/...` with an `x-api-key` header (`API_KEY` env var), and pass the backend's status/body straight through on error (don't swallow or reshape backend errors — see `src/app/api/games/route.ts` for the pattern). All sports data comes from the backend; this repo makes no direct ESPN calls.

content.text: Also updating the backend CLAUDE.md league list, then fixing the generation pipeline:

result.output:
30	- `service/EspnService` — builds ESPN URLs (`{base}/{sport}/{league}/scoreboard` and `.../summary?event={id}`) and fetches via `RestTemplate`. League/sport path segments come from the `League` enum.
31	- `service/GameServiceImpl` — persists fetched games and runs a `@Scheduled(fixedRate = 20000)` job (`updateGamesForToday`) that checks `GameRepository.shouldUpdate()` (any game live or due to start in the last 24h) before re-fetching ESPN data for every league.
32	- `service/CalendarServiceImpl` — computes "current" league context (year/seasonType/week/date) via a native query (`GameRepository.findCurrentContext`) that prioritizes in-progress games, then next upcoming, then most recent final. Result is cached in `currentContext` (key = league + timezone), evicted every 5 minutes (`CacheConfig`).
33	- `enums/League` — single source of truth for supported leagues (`nfl`, `college-football`, `nhl`, `usa.1`) and their capability flags (`supportsYearFilter`, `supportsWeekFilter`, `supportsStandings`, `contextMode` — `"season"` vs `"date"` — `statsProfile`, `teamOrder`, `supportsOdds`, `supportsLiveDetails`). Controllers and services branch on these flags instead of hardcoding per-league logic.
34	- `config/ApiKeyAuthFilter` + `config/SecurityConfig` — stateless `x-api-key` header check via a filter placed before Spring Security's auth filter; CORS is locked to `https://tallyo.us` (update `SecurityConfig` if the frontend origin changes).
35	- `exception/GlobalExceptionHandler` — all errors return the same `ApiError` envelope (`code`, `message`, `details?`, `path?`, `timestamp?`). `InvalidRequestException` → 400; `DateTimeException` → 400 `INVALID_TIMEZONE`; `RestClientException`/`ResourceAccessException` (ESPN upstream failures) → 502; everything else → 500.
36	
37	### Timezone/date handling

input.old_string: - `enums/League` — single source of truth for supported leagues (`nfl`, `college-football`, `nhl`, `usa.1`) and their capability flags

---
name: world-cup-prod-backfill
description: World Cup league shipped 2026-07-05; prod may still need the one-time stats backfill POST for already-played matches
metadata:
  type: project
---

World Cup support (`world_cup` league, ESPN slug `fifa.world`) merged to both repos on 2026-07-05 (backend PR #3, frontend commit "Add world cup scores"). The 2026 tournament runs 2026-06-11 to 2026-07-19.

Matches played before the deploy land via the schedule-only refresh as finals with no leaders/scoring plays/box scores, and the 20s live job never revisits finished games. Unless already done, run once against prod:

`POST /api/v1/games?league=world_cup&shouldFetchStats=true` (with `x-api-key`; took ~37s for all 104 games locally).

Verify with `GET /api/v1/games/<gameId>/details` for a pre-deploy match returning non-empty leaders/scoringPlays.

# Memory Index

- [World Cup prod backfill](world-cup-prod-backfill.md) — one-time stats backfill POST may still be pending on prod after the 2026-07-05 World Cup launch

result.output: File created successfully at: /Users/jackpotter/.claude/projects/-Users-jackpotter-projects-tallyo-backend/memory/MEMORY.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.text:
Both done.

**API generation fixed.** The root cause: the committed `api.generated.ts` was always the generator's output run through prettier, but `check:api-contract` diffed against the generator's *raw* output — so the check could never pass. I proved the relationship first (generating from the old spec + `prettier --write` reproduces the committed file byte-for-byte), then made the pipeline match reality: `gen:api-types` in `package.json` now pipes the generated file through prettier. `npm run check:api-contract` now passes for real, and it will still catch genuine spec/type drift. Verified the run is idempotent (identical checksum before/after regeneration) and typecheck is clean.

**CLAUDE.md updated in both repos.** In the frontend, I fixed the claims I'd verified were stale: removed the nonexistent `/api/stats` route and the `src/lib/espn/espnService.ts` layer (everything goes through the backend proxy now — no direct ESPN calls), corrected the statDisplayMaps path to `src/lib/leagues/`, and replaced the inaccurate "standings force-enabled for nhl/mls" note with the actually-useful fact that `LEAGUE_ID_REGEX`/`parseLeagueId` gates all league routing. In the backend, the `League` enum description now includes `fifa.world`. I also updated the frontend verify skill so it no longer documents the workaround for the bug we just fixed.

One thing I noticed along the way: you merged both World Cup PRs between turns (backend PR #3 auto-deploys on push to main). Reminder from yesterday's verification — the ~50 matches played before the deploy will have no leaders/scoring plays/box scores until a one-time backfill runs against prod: `POST /api/v1/games?league=world_cup&shouldFetchStats=true`. I've noted that in memory in case it hasn't happened yet.

Pending changes: frontend `CLAUDE.md` + `package.json` + the skill file (on `master` — I'd branch before committing), backend `CLAUDE.md` (on `fix/prune-stale-scheduled-games`).
What curl command can I run to force update the world cup games?

Against prod:

```bash
curl -X POST "https://api.tallyo.us/api/v1/games?league=world_cup&shouldFetchStats=true" \
  -H "x-api-key: $API_KEY"
```

Use the prod API key (the frontend's `.env.local`/Vercel `API_KEY` value, or `API_KEY` from the backend host's `.env`). It re-fetches all 104 World Cup games from ESPN and attaches box scores, leaders, and scoring plays — took about 37 seconds locally, and it's idempotent, so running it again is harmless. You'll get back `{"gameCount":104,"durationMs":...}` when it finishes.

To confirm it worked, pick a match that finished before the deploy (e.g. Morocco–Canada from yesterday) and check details are populated:

```bash
curl "https://api.tallyo.us/api/v1/games/760502/details" -H "x-api-key: $API_KEY"
```

Non-empty `leaders` and `scoringPlays` means the backfill landed.
Give me the full request, including the api key

input.command: grep "^API_KEY=" /Users/jackpotter/projects/tallyo/.env.local | cut -d= -f2
input.description: Read API key from frontend .env.local

content.text:
Here's the full command:

```bash
curl -X POST "https://api.tallyo.us/api/v1/games?league=world_cup&shouldFetchStats=true" \
  -H "x-api-key: REDACTED"
```

That's the key from the frontend's `.env.local` (the one the deployed UI uses against prod). Expect it to run ~40 seconds and return `{"gameCount":104,"durationMs":...}`.

And to verify the backfill took, check a pre-deploy match has details:

```bash
curl "https://api.tallyo.us/api/v1/games/760502/details" \
  -H "x-api-key: REDACTED"
```
The world cup standings aren't displaying right. Within the groups they look weird

Let me look at what the standings actually return for a group — I noticed earlier that Group A came back as Mexico(9 pts), Czechia(1), South Korea(3), South Africa(4), which isn't table order. Let me dig into the mapper and the ESPN payload.

content.text:
Fixed — it was a backend sorting bug, and the fix is verified.

**Root cause:** ESPN returns World Cup group standings entries in arbitrary order but includes a `rank` stat per team. The backend's `EspnStandingsMapper` sorted teams by `seed`, which soccer payloads don't have — so every team defaulted to the same sort key and groups rendered in ESPN's raw payload order (e.g. Group A showed Mexico, Czechia, South Korea, South Africa instead of Mexico 9pts, South Africa 4, South Korea 3, Czechia 1). The frontend has no default sort of its own, so it faithfully displayed the scrambled order.

**Fix:** `EspnStandingsMapper.parseConference` now sorts by the `rank` stat when a team has a positive one, falling back to `seed` otherwise (`EspnStandingsMapper.java:142` plus a small `sortRank` helper).

**Verified against the live API** (local backend, real ESPN data):
- All World Cup groups now in table order — Group A: Mexico(1), South Africa(2), South Korea(3), Czechia(4); tiebreaks match ESPN's ranking too (Group B: Canada above Bosnia at 4pts each).
- **Bonus: this also fixed MLS**, which turned out to have the same latent bug — its tables were in payload order (Chicago, Columbus, New England…) while ESPN's ranks say Nashville 1, Inter Miami 2, Chicago 3. It just hadn't been noticed.
- No regression for NHL/NFL: their payloads have no `rank` stat, so they take the exact same seed/pre-sorted path as before (confirmed both endpoints return unchanged orderings).

The change is uncommitted in the backend repo (one file, `EspnStandingsMapper.java`, on `main` — I'd branch before committing). Since pushing to `main` auto-deploys prod, say the word and I'll commit/PR it.
